learnings.test_name: Namespace cleanup cascade — ConflictError 409 on all retry runs
learnings.root_cause: Test teardown/cleanup did not delete Kubernetes namespaces when the first run failed mid-setup (fixture errors prevent teardown code from running). On all retry runs, pytest attempts to re-create those namespaces and hits ConflictError 409 AlreadyExists: 'test-guardrails-hf-upgrade' (run 2) and 'upgrade-model-server' (run 3). This prevents any retry from even reaching the actual test logic, compounding every underlying failure.
learnings.recommendation: 1) Add a pre-test namespace-cleanup step to the pipeline or use unique per-run namespace suffixes. 2) Implement a finalizer/cleanup job that runs unconditionally after each test execution, regardless of fixture setup failures. 3) Consider using ocp_resources ResourceEdit's context managers consistently so teardown always runs. 4) Use --forked-ns or randomized namespace names to avoid collisions between pipeline retries.
